text: The surgeon isolates the right posterior hepatic artery using a vessel loop, followed by portal vein dissection. Blunt dissection with suction irrigation reveals the left hepatic artery and surrounding lymphatic vessels, which are skeletonized, double-clipped, and divided using Hemlock clips. The right anterior hepatic artery undergoes the same procedure. The portal vein dissection is completed to the bifurcation, with the left portal vein visible transversely and the caudate lobe branch individually clipped and divided.

The surgeon uses robotic scissors to sharply divide the double clips on the patient's side. The dissection of the hepatic duct is underway, focusing on the common bile duct at the bifurcation with the left hepatic duct. The manipulation of the hepatic duct at this proximal bifurcation appears visually challenging.

After completing the dissection of the portal hepatitis and ligating the left hepatic duct, portal vein, and arteries, the surgeon performs the Pringle maneuver. The liver's demarcation is confirmed with ultrasound to ensure negative margins. The parenchymal dissection is carried out using blunt dissection, a robotic stapler, vascular load, and vessel sealer, with vessels being individually clipped as encountered. The stapler is shown moving smoothly without resistance, indicating proper technique. The surgeon periodically releases the Pringle maneuver to maintain liver perfusion. The metal hepatic vein is visible during the procedure.

The surgeon identifies, double clips, and divides the right branch of the right hepatic vein at Segment eight, near the junction with the inferior vena cava. A small hole in the middle hepatic vein is repaired with proline suture. The parenchymal transection is completed using a clip on the patient side and a vessel sealer on the specimen side. The liver parenchyma appears normal, and the specimen is extracted using a 15mm endoscopic bag through the system port. The transection surface is inspected for bile leaks, and hemostatic agents are applied as needed.
